criteria.label: Choose between `sorted` (new list) and `list.sort` (in-place) intentionally.
criteria.example: `fresh = sorted(rows, key=...)` vs `rows.sort(key=...)` — pick by whether you need to preserve the original.

criteria.label: Sort by a key (with negation and tuple keys for direction and multi-key ordering).
Use stability to chain multi-key sorts cleanly.
Pick the right algorithm by need: Timsort default, mergesort for stable+worst-case, heapsort for in-place, radix/counting for bounded keys.
criteria.example: Out-of-core sort of 100 GB of logs → external mergesort. Top-k of streaming scores → heapq.

criteria.label: Use heapq.nlargest / nsmallest instead of full-sort + slice when k ≪ n.
criteria.example: `heapq.nlargest(10, chunks, key=lambda c: c.score)` — O(n log k).

criteria.label: Avoid O(n² log n) trap of re-sorting on every insert in a streaming loop.
criteria.example: Use `sortedcontainers.SortedList` (O(log n) per insert) instead of `xs.append(); xs.sort()` in a loop.
